title: BGSpec Upper-Floor Node
description: Defines a single node on any floor above Floor 0 (floors 1, 2, 3, …). Upper-floor nodes are human-authored and carry no closed BGSpec taxonomy (no bgKind, infraProvider, or provider blocks). Visual identity is expressed only through Cytoscape-compatible shape and optional inline visual hints. Semantic meaning (service names, business processes, etc.) lives in label and meta — not in schema-enforced types.

id.description: Globally unique node identifier within this BGSpec document.

label.description: Human-readable display name. Author-defined; not validated against any taxonomy.

floor.description: Floor number. Must be >= 1 and must match the containing floor object's floor field.

shape.description: Cytoscape node body shape. This is the only schema-required visual discriminator for upper-floor nodes.

parent.description: ID of another node on the same floor used as a Cytoscape compound parent. For compound parents, prefer rectangle-family shapes per Cytoscape constraints.

refs.description: Optional IDs of nodes on floor (this.floor - 1) that this node composes or traces to. Traceability rules are enforced by the application (see SPECS.md); the schema does not require refs on every node.

meta.description: Free-form author metadata (team, docs, tags, business notes). No closed vocabulary.

examples.comment: Floor 1 — logical service grouping a Floor 0 deployment
Floor 2 — product capability composed from Floor 1 services
Floor 3 — business workflow with no strict lower-floor refs (leaf allowed per SPECS.md)
Compound group on an upper floor
